rows.title: Stanley Cup Final · Game 6
rows.meta: Carolina at Vegas · ABC · Canes can clinch (lead 3–2)

rows.title: UFC Freedom 250 · White House

sections.name: NHL · Cup Final
sections.ctx: Carolina leads 3–2 and can lift its first Cup since 2006 tonight in Vegas. A Knights win forces Game 7.

links.text: Full Cup Final schedule, TV & results
How to watch Game 6: time, channel, stream

notes: Game 5 recap: Svechnikov scored twice and Ehlers piled up three assists in Carolina's 4–2 win at home.

sections.name: Boston Bruins
sections.ctx: Out in Round 1 to Buffalo. Draft June 26–27 (No. 23 overall); free agency opens July 1.

links.text: Should the B's chase Rasmus Andersson?
Two moves the Bruins must make this summer

notes: Sweeney is hunting top-four, right-shot D. Per Shinzawa, Boston wants both Andersson and Raddysh — but a crowded market could push the price past their limit.
Andersson (with Vegas in the Final) is a pending UFA; the expected Knights extension is still unsigned, though LeBrun thinks a deal stays likely.
Raddysh is coming off a career year in Tampa (22–48–70). Either blueliner likely lands a $7.5M–$9.5M AAV.
Extensions done: Reichel (1 yr / $950K) and Mutter (1 yr two-way / $850K), both through 2026–27.
War chest: ~$16.4M cap space (cap up to $104M) and five first-round picks over the next three drafts.

sections.name: New England Patriots
sections.ctx: Reigning AFC champs (lost SB LX to Seattle). Minicamp wrapped June 11; camp vets report July 24.

links.text: Winners & losers from the final minicamp practice

notes: A.J. Brown is clicking with Maye — two TD grabs on the final minicamp day, including the play of the day in the back corner.
Last session ended early after Maye got tangled with rusher Bradyn Swinson; both fine.
NFL closed its Stefon Diggs review with no discipline (insufficient evidence).
Christian Gonzalez contract talks ongoing; skipped voluntary work, due at mandatory dates.
Schedule: opens at Seattle (SB LX rematch); Week 10 in Germany vs. Detroit; five primetime games.

sections.name: Ohio State Buckeyes
sections.ctx: A top title contender for 2026. Last year ended with a Big Ten title-game loss to champ Indiana and a 24–14 Cotton Bowl loss to Miami. New OC Arthur Smith; black uniforms back.

links.text: Smith and Sayin both carry legit Heisman odds
McElroy ranks Sayin the No. 1 QB for 2026

notes: Jeremiah Smith tops most way-too-early Heisman boards (2,500+ yds, 27 TDs in two years); back for year three.
Julian Sayin sits atop McElroy's preseason QB rankings after a 3,600-yd, 32-TD, Big Ten Freshman of the Year debut.
New OC Arthur Smith has the offense buzzing; Sayin says the program's carrying "a chip on its shoulder."
Black uniforms return for the first time since 2022.

sections.ctx: A historic night — the Octagon lands on the South Lawn of the White House.
